name: Signet Developer SDK
description: Developer-facing SDK for external service integration with four core primitives: verify, requestCapability, checkAuthority, and parseCredential.

types.name: Claim

fields.name: attribute
fields.description: The attributed claimed by the proof.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The value associated with the attributed claimed.

types.description: Represents a claim consisting of an attribute and its associated value.

types.name: CapabilitySpec

fields.name: expiration
fields.description: Expiration time of the capability in Unix timestamp.

types.description: Specifies a capability by describing its permissions and expiration.

types.name: SignetId

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the Signet identity.

types.description: Represents a unique identifier for a Signet identity.

types.name: SignetError
types.description: Possible errors that may occur during SDK operations.

functions.name: verify
functions.description: Verifies a Signet proof against a claimed attribute.

error_cases.name: InvalidProof
error_cases.condition: Proof data format is invalid or corrupted.

error_cases.name: InvalidClaim
error_cases.condition: The claim does not match the verified proof.

postconditions: Returns true if the proof is valid for the claim, otherwise false.

functions.name: requestCapability
functions.description: Requests a scoped capability from a Signet MCP server.

error_cases.name: CapabilityRequestFailed
error_cases.condition: Request to MCP server failed due to network issues or invalid spec.

postconditions: Returns a capability token if request is successful.

functions.name: checkAuthority
functions.description: Checks whether a Signet identity possesses a specified authority.

error_cases.name: AuthorityCheckFailed
error_cases.condition: The authority check could not be completed.

postconditions: Returns true if the identity has authority, otherwise false.

functions.name: parseCredential
functions.description: Parses and validates an SD-JWT or BBS+ credential.

error_cases.name: CredentialParseError
error_cases.condition: Token is not valid or cannot be parsed.

preconditions: Token should be a valid SD-JWT or BBS+ format.
postconditions: Returns a claim if token is successfully parsed and validated.

invariants: All operations must return consistent results given the same input.
Tokens and metadata must remain confidential and not be stored in plaintext.
